_comment: Endpoint-class adverse-direction registry (F1e) for NOAEL gate C7 (bidirectional adverse-direction with corroboration triggers). Schema captures: (a) primary adverse direction per endpoint class, (b) bidirectional corroboration triggers when the opposite direction can also be adverse. Cross-domain triggers reference findings at the same dose level on other endpoints.
_status: draft - awaiting toxicologist sign-off per DATA-GAP-NOAEL-ALG-02; entries to be expanded as C7 corroboration matching predicate (RG-NOAEL-ALG-14) is implemented

BW.rationale: Decreased body weight or impaired weight gain is the canonical adverse signal in repeat-dose toxicology (palatability, systemic toxicity, malabsorption).

triggers.rationale: Hyperphagia indication — feed consumption increase corroborates BW-up as pharmacologically driven.
Fluid retention / edema clinical signs corroborate BW-up as fluid-driven.
Organomegaly or fat-depot increase corroborates BW-up as adipogenic / hypertrophic.
Per Kale 2022 / Cusi 2010 / Nesto 2003 — class effect via fluid retention + adipogenesis.
Per Allison 1999 meta-analysis / Kale 2022 — metabolic syndrome induction.
Per Schacke 2002 / Kale 2022 — fluid retention + fat redistribution.

FW.rationale: Decreased food consumption is canonical adverse signal (palatability, systemic toxicity, gastrointestinal effects).

triggers.rationale: Hyperphagia is a recognized class effect of atypical antipsychotics.

OM.rationale: Organomegaly (organ-weight increase) is more commonly the adverse signal (hypertrophy, hyperplasia, edema, infiltrate). Atrophy (decrease) is also adverse but lower base rate at typical dose levels.

triggers.rationale: Macroscopic atrophy at the same organ corroborates organ-weight-decrease as toxicity.
Microscopic atrophy / depletion at the same organ corroborates organ-weight-decrease as toxicity.
Necrosis at the same organ corroborates organ-weight-decrease via tissue loss.

CL_incidence.rationale: Increased clinical-sign incidence (any direction in the underlying observation) is the adverse signal — incidence is unidirectional by construction.

DS_incidence.rationale: Increased mortality / morbidity is the adverse signal — incidence is unidirectional by construction.

LB_per_analyte._comment: Lab biomarkers — direction adversity is per-analyte and depends on the canonical biomarker. Defer to lab-clinical-catalog (frontend) and shared/rules/lab-clinical-rules.json (backend). This entry signals that LB endpoints should consult the per-analyte registry, not a single endpoint-class direction.
LB_per_analyte.rationale: Lab analytes vary: ALT/AST up = adverse; albumin down = adverse; WBC bidirectional. The per-analyte direction is encoded in lab-clinical-rules.json + lab-clinical-catalog.ts.
